Race screen — 2026-07-20 · updated 16:36

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 4/20 (20%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 5/20 (25%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-07-19 · updated 09:09

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 8/13 (62%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 8/13 (62%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-07-18 · updated 09:02

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 11/34 (32%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 17/34 (50%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-07-17 · updated 09:02

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 22/31 (71%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 14/31 (45%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-07-16 · updated 09:02

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 12/24 (50%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 16/24 (67%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-07-15 · updated 09:02

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 10/18 (56%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 13/18 (72%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-07-14 · updated 09:02

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 15/20 (75%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 14/20 (70%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-07-13 · updated 09:03

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 11/21 (52%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 12/21 (57%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-07-12 · updated 09:01

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 4/9 (44%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 5/9 (56%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-07-11 · updated 09:02

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 19/33 (58%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 13/33 (39%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-07-10 · updated 09:01

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 14/27 (52%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 10/27 (37%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-07-09 · updated 09:02

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 11/23 (48%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 12/23 (52%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-07-08 · updated 09:02

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 10/16 (62%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 9/16 (56%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded

Decision support, not betting advice. Prices are forecast/early — check the live market. Speed figures are final-time only (no sectionals), so weigh them with your own read on how a race was run.

Race screen — 2026-07-07 · updated 09:02

Order: each race is in market order (shortest forecast price first). A gold ◆ 1/2/3 marks your data model's top 3 — a gold pick at a longer price is where your data disagrees with the market (a value spot for your eye).
Badges by the name: M2 Market #2 — 2nd favourite, qualifying race, morning price 3.5–8.0 (proven +15% ROI strategy)   ★ EYE finished last run strongly (+score)   ⚠ hard trk easy→hard track step-up (−score)   ▼ easy trk recently placed at much tougher track (+score)   ⚠ draw badly drawn here (−score)   ⚠ pace run-style fights the course pace bias (−score)   ⚠ ground has tried this going but never placed on it (−score)   ▼ cls dropping in class today (+score)   ▲ cls rising in class today (−score)   ▼ in money in vs opening show   ▲ drift drifting vs opening show   ⚠ 2 runs / IRE form too little / foreign form to read.
OR column: ↑ green arrow = BHA rating rising (improving horse); ↓ orange = rating falling.   TF column: Timeform stars 1–5 (★★★★★ = top rated).
Suit column: going✓ / trip✓ / trk✓ (track toughness) / draw✓ (well drawn).
Rest: green Crse = won there; yellow comment = trouble in running; greyed = novice/bumper or non-handicap 5–6f sprint (left out of the tally — handicap sprints now shown at full opacity). Res fills in after racing (winner green; ✓ = winner came from the top-3 market rows, ◆ = one of your gold data picks won); Spd is a rough home figure; BSP fills in the next morning.
Winner in the market top 3: 13/19 (68%)  ·  in your gold data top 3: 13/19 (68%) — greyed novice / sprint races excluded
